title: Gen Digital (GEN) Intrinsic Value
description: Discounted cash flow (DCF) intrinsic value analysis of Gen Digital Inc. (GEN).

fcfGrowth.title: FCF Growth Rate (Y1-Y5)
fcfGrowth.rationale: A 10.0% growth rate assumes continued robust execution on cross-selling initiatives and the realization of cost synergies, driving strong double-digit free cash flow expansion.

discountRate.title: Discount Rate (WACC)
discountRate.rationale: A 9.5% discount rate reflects the stable, recurring nature of the consumer software subscription model, offset slightly by the intense competitive environment.

terminalGrowth.title: Terminal Growth Rate
terminalGrowth.rationale: 2.0% is a conservative terminal growth rate for a mature consumer software business operating in a saturated market.

thesisTitle: The Cyber Safety Software Value Play
thesisParagraphs: Gen Digital represents a compelling value proposition in the software sector, distinguished by its massive installed base and consistent generation of significant free cash flow. The company's portfolio of recognizable brands—including Norton, Avast, and LifeLock—provides a comprehensive suite of cyber safety solutions that benefit from high user retention rates.

The intrinsic value calculation suggests that the market is significantly undervaluing the durability of this recurring revenue stream. While user growth may be measured in a mature market, the company's ability to drive margin expansion through operational efficiencies and strategic cross-selling presents a clear path to sustained, long-term shareholder value creation.
verdictFooter: Based on FY2025 actuals. Data via S&P Global (Mar 20, 2026).

faq.question: Why is the calculated intrinsic value so much higher than the current price?
faq.answer: The model heavily weights the company's strong, predictable free cash flow generation, which the broader market may be discounting due to concerns over slow user growth or competition from built-in OS security features.

faq.question: What is the key driver of the 10.0% growth assumption?
faq.answer: The growth assumption is predicated on the successful execution of cross-selling strategies—moving users from basic antivirus products to comprehensive, higher-margin bundles that include identity protection and VPN services.

faq.question: How do acquisitions factor into this analysis?
faq.answer: Historically, Gen Digital has grown through significant acquisitions. The model assumes the successful integration of these assets and the realization of promised cost synergies, which structurally improve long-term free cash flow margins.
